mission.title: kubevirt: USB host pass through
mission.description:
**What this PR does / why we need it**:
This PR allows USB devices that are plugged to the cluster's Nodes to be allocated to VMs running in said Nodes.

### Staring with main components and a simple use case

A `cluster admin` can define USB devices he wants to expose to VM using KubeVirt CRD, under permitted host devices
A new CRD called NodeConfig is introduce to allow requiring USB devices.  Note that [enabling](https://kubevirt.io/user-guide/operations/activating_feature_gates/) `HostDevice

steps.title: Understand the problem
steps.description:
**What this PR does / why we need it**:
This PR allows USB devices that are plugged to the cluster's Nodes to be allocated to VMs running in said Nodes.

### Staring with main components and a simple

steps.title: Apply the configuration
steps.description:
Apply the following configuration to your cluster:
```yaml
apiVersion: kubevirt.io/v1
kind: KubeVirt
metadata:
  name: kubevirt
  namespace: kubevirt
spec:
  configuration:
    permittedHostDevices:
      usb:
        - resourceName: kubevirt.io/storage
          selectors:
            - vendor: "46f4"
              product: "0001"
    developerConfiguration: 
      featureGates:
        - HostDevices
```

steps.title: Review the fix
steps.description: The fix was implemented in https://github.com/kubevirt/project-infra/pull/2872. Review the changes to understand the solution.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
This is possible since:
  https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirtci/pull/996

The follow CI would benefit from it:
  https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/pull/10015
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: kubevirt.io/v1
kind: KubeVirt
metadata:
  name: kubevirt
  namespace: kubevirt
spec:
  configuration:
    permittedHostDevices:
      usb:
        - resourceName: kubevirt.io/storage
          selectors:
            - vendor: "46f4"
              product: "0001"
    developerConfiguration: 
      featureGates:
        - HostDevices

apiVersion: kubevirt.io/v1
kind: KubeVirt
metadata:
  name: kubevirt
  namespace: kubevirt
spec:
  configuration:
    permittedHostDevices:
      usb:
        - resourceName: kubevirt.io/storage
          selectors:
            - vendor: "46f4"
              product: "0001"
    developerConfiguration: 
      featureGates:
        - HostDevices

spec:
  domain:
    devices:
      hostDevices:
      - deviceName: kubevirt.io/storage
        name: my-storage-usb-devices

(toso) $ export KUBEVIRT_PROVIDER=k8s-1.26-centos9
(toso) $ export KUBEVIRT_TAG=latest
(toso) $ export KUBEVIRT_PROVIDER_EXTRA_ARGS="--usb 20M --usb 30M --usb 40M"
(toso) $ make cluster-up
...
(toso) $ ./cluster-up/ssh.sh node01
(node01) $ dmesg | grep -i idVendor=46f4
[    1.204437] usb 4-1: New USB device found, idVendor=46f4, idProduct=0001, bcdDevice= 0.00
[    1.204501] usb 3-1: New USB device found, idVendor=46f4, idProduct=0001, bcdDevice= 0.00
[    1.330900] usb 4-2: New USB device found, idVendor=46f4, idProduct=0001, bcdDevice= 0.00

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
